title: parthenium confertum var. typicum
intro: Parthenium confertum var. typicum, commonly known as the hairy fleabane, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as a diuretic, which helps to flush out excess fluids from the body. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Parthenium confertum var. typicum are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
